channel.title: Music on RaihnForge
channel.description: Recent content in Music on RaihnForge

item.title: Legends of Rock: Michael Jackson and Bob Dylan
item.description:
Introduction

Some stars are born of their time and accentuate the moment. They burn brightly and pass into memory, leaving their mark on culture. Some artists rise to such stardom that they become the marks of culture, maintaining relevance over decades and generations across genres. Michael Jackson and Bob Dylan are such artists, so keenly talented. To explore the staying power of these legends of rock, each artist’s early successes are compared with later achievements that span their careers over decades. We may discover how these two preserved and shared their authentic artistic evolution over time while maintaining a sensibility toward cultural shifts, adapting new technologies, and responding to genre evolutions while meeting industry demands.

item.title: The Emergence of Punk: A Reaction to the Late 1970s Mainstream
item.description:
Mainstream Excess as the Catalyst

Punk was a reaction to the mainstream excess and perceived inauthenticity of the hippie aesthetic, which had become highly produced and esoteric. It rebelled against the intricate, polished tracks that dominated Album-Oriented Rock (AOR). These investor-backed, DJ-minimized FM stations prioritized shorter, safer tracks to maximize advertising revenue. As investors focused more on securing profits, mainstream rock music became increasingly commercial and conservative.

The punk scene revived a do-it-yourself (DIY), authentic, edgy, garage-band style, rejecting the notion that highly produced recording studios were necessary. It also reintroduced raw, rebellious lyrics, pushing back against the commercial conservatism that had become artistically antithetical to rock’s identity.
